file_name: BOLIVARMININGCORP_05_23_2003-EX-2.1-VISP WEB SITE BUILDING AND HOSTING AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall renew automatically each year thereafter, unless either party serves written notice of its intention not to renew, on the other at least 90 days prior to the expiration of the then current term of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall renew automatically each year thereafter, unless either party serves written notice of its intention to renew, on the other at least 90 days prior to the expiration of the then current term of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The word 'not' has been removed from 'not to renew'. This introduces a contradiction because Section 1 now implies that the agreement automatically renews unless a party gives notice *to* renew, which is illogical and contradicts the initial statement of automatic renewal. This creates confusion about the renewal process.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1 TERM This agreement shall be for a term of five years from the date of this document. This agreement shall renew automatically each year thereafter, unless either party serves written notice of its intention to renew, on the other at least 90 days prior to the expiration of the then current term of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'not' from 'not to renew' creates a direct contradiction within the contract. The original text implies automatic renewal unless notice *not* to renew is given. The changed text implies automatic renewal unless notice *to* renew is given, which is illogical and creates confusion about the renewal process. This contradicts the term that the agreement will automatically renew without any action, creating ambiguity and thus a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, either party may assign this Agreement, without the prior consent of the other party, to any person, partnership, firm or corporation affiliated by common ownership with the assigning party, acquiring all or substantially all of such party's assets or, in the case of PROVIDER, acquiring any assets of PROVIDER associated with its wholesale Internet access and services business.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, neither party may assign this Agreement, without the prior consent of the other party, to any person, partnership, firm or corporation affiliated by common ownership with the assigning party, acquiring all or substantially all of such party's assets or, in the case of PROVIDER, acquiring any assets of PROVIDER associated with its wholesale Internet access and services business.
perturbation.explanation: The word 'either' has been changed to 'neither.' This change introduces an in-text contradiction with the first portion of Section 8, where it states that “This Agreement shall be binding upon the parties and their respective successors and assigns”. The original allows assignment in specific circumstances (affiliated companies) without consent but now prohibits any assignment to affiliated companies without consent, even though the start of section 8 states it is binding upon the parties. This creates confusion and uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8 ASSIGNMENT This Agreement shall be binding upon the parties and their respective successors and assigns; provided, however, that no rights or obligations hereunder, including but not limited to Licensee's Subscriber accounts, shall be assigned or transferred, in whole or in part, by either of the parties hereto to any person, firm or corporation without prior written consent by the other party, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or delayed. Notwithstanding the foregoing, neither party may assign this Agreement, without the prior consent of the other party, to any person, partnership, firm or corporation affiliated by common ownership with the assigning party, acquiring all or substantially all of such party's assets or, in the case of PROVIDER, acquiring any assets of PROVIDER associated with its wholesale Internet access and services business.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'either' to 'neither' introduces a strong in-text contradiction. Originally, the agreement allowed assignment to affiliated entities without consent under specific conditions. The revised text now prohibits such assignments entirely without prior consent, directly conflicting with the initial statement in Section 8 that the agreement binds successors and assigns. This creates a clear contradiction, as the agreement is no longer unconditionally binding on assigns in cases of affiliated entities. The contradicted text is located in the same location as the Changed Text: “Notwithstanding the foregoing, neither party may assign this Agreement, without the prior consent of the other party, to any person, partnership, firm or corporation affiliated by common ownership with the assigning party, acquiring all or substantially all of such party's assets or, in the case of PROVIDER, acquiring any assets of PROVIDER associated with its wholesale Internet access and services business.”

perturbation.original_text: Any award rendered by the arbitrator shall be binding and enforceable by any party to the arbitration and judgment shall be rendered upon it in a court of competent subject matter jurisdiction located in New York.
perturbation.changed_text: Any award rendered by the arbitrator shall be non-binding and unenforceable by any party to the arbitration and judgment shall be rendered upon it in a court of competent subject matter jurisdiction located in New York.
perturbation.explanation: The word 'binding' has been changed to 'non-binding'. This creates an in-text contradiction by stating the arbitration decision is not final. However, the text leaves open the possibility for it being enforced through court, which raises uncertainty of the impact of the arbitration clause of Section 11.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11 CONTROLLING LAW AND ARBITRATION All questions regarding the validity, interpretation, performance and enforcement of the provisions of this Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the state of New York. Any controversy or claim arising out of, relating to or in connection with this Agreement that has not been resolved through the informal mediation of the parties shall be resolved through arbitration pursuant to the Commercial Arbitration Rules of the American Arbitration Association then in effect, as modified by the terms set forth below: (a) the arbitration shall be conducted in New York state; (b) the arbitration shall be conducted by a single arbitrator selected by the parties; (c) the parties shall act in a commercially reasonable manner and speedily select and then conduct the arbitration within 45 days with the expenditure of minimal discovery efforts and expense which shall be determined, if necessary, by the arbitrator. The agreement to arbitrate shall be specifically enforceable under prevailing New York law. Any award rendered by the arbitrator shall be non-binding and unenforceable by any party to the arbitration and judgment shall be rendered upon it in a court of competent subject matter jurisdiction located in New York.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the arbitrator's award is 'binding and enforceable'. The changed text alters this to 'non-binding and unenforceable'. This directly contradicts the purpose of arbitration, which is to provide a final and binding resolution. The contradicted text, which now includes the changed text, states that the arbitration award is 'non-binding and unenforceable', which is a direct contradiction with the general understanding and legal purpose of arbitration agreements, particularly when the original text indicates arbitration is intended to resolve disputes definitively. The explicit statement that the award is non-binding creates an in-text contradiction with any implied or explicit expectation of finality or enforceability that would be expected within an arbitration clause in a contract.
